channel.title: NYT > Movies
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Mourning After the Michael Jackson Movie? It Might Be ‘Michosis.’
item.description: Some Michael Jackson fans are experiencing deep, lingering grief after watching the biopic — a potent reminder that he is gone, they say.
item.dc:creator: Farah Fleurima
item.media:description: Jaafar Jackson as Michael Jackson in the recent biopic “Michael,” which has set off a wave of renewed mourning over the pop star’s death.

item.title: With ‘Girls Like Girls,’ Hayley Kiyoko Turns Her Teenage Pain Into Art
item.description: The once-closeted star has reinvented her song “Girls Like Girls” as a best-selling Y.A. novel and a new theatrical film. It wasn’t easy.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Spencer
item.media:credit: Jennelle Fong for The New York Times
item.media:description: Hayley Kiyoko in Los Angeles. Her feature directing debut, “Girls Like Girls,” is in theaters.

item.title: ‘Obsession’ Is a Surprise Blockbuster. Who Gets the Profits?
item.description: As the breakout horror hit crosses $300 million at the global box office, its art director set off a debate about just compensation for crew members.
item.dc:creator: Malia Mendez

item.title: ‘The Moment,’ ‘Daddio’ and More Streaming Gems
item.description: A pair of unconventional takes to the life of a musician bookend this month’s under-the-radar recommendations for your subscription streaming services.
item.dc:creator: Jason Bailey
item.category: Swamp Dogg Gets His Pool Painted (Movie)

item.title: Excited for World Cup? Stream 14 Great Soccer Movies.
item.description: The beautiful game is the inspiring setting for films from countries large (Brazil) and small (Cape Verde). Watch them while the World Cup is in full swing.
item.dc:creator: Carlos Aguilar
item.category: What Do We See When We Look at the Sky? (Movie)
item.media:description: Keira Knightley, left, and Parminder Nagra in “Bend It Like Beckham.”

item.title: ‘Toy Story 5’ Fuels Hollywood’s Hottest Summer Since 2019
item.description: The Disney-Pixar sequel was expected to make $160 million in North America over the weekend, lifting the summer box office to $1.85 billion to date.
item.dc:creator: Brooks Barnes

item.title: Joan Cusack, the Actress Behind Jessie in ‘Toy Story,’ Takes the Lead
item.description: After 27 years, the beloved cowgirl doll voiced by Cusack is finally in charge, but the actress played an important role in shaping the character early on.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bahr
item.media:credit: Arsenii Vaselenko for The New York Times
item.media:description: Cusack stepped away from acting to be a parent: “I wanted to be around and raise my kids, and if you’re acting, you always have to go away to work.”

item.title: The Dad Movie Canon: Films That Define Dad Cinema
item.description: What is it about a certain kind of film that appeals to fathers? Our writer took a stab at defining Dad Cinema and rounding up its greatest hits.
item.dc:creator: Jason Bailey
item.category: Master and Commander: The Far Side of the World (Movie)
item.media:credit: Clockwise from top left: Michael Weinstein/Castle Rock Entertainment; The Mirisch Company; Orion Pictures; Stephen Vaughan/Warner Bros.; Paramount Pictures/Moviepix, via Getty Images; Stephen Vaughan/20th Century Fox; Warner Bros.

item.title: ‘Toy Story 5’ Review: The Machines Have Come for the Children
item.description: In this latest installment of the popular franchise, Woody and Buzz Lightyear are back and must contend with Bonnie’s mesmerizing new toy.
item.dc:creator: Manohla Dargis

item.title: ‘In the Hand of Dante’ Review: A Not So Divine Folly
item.description: Not even a double dose of Oscar Isaac can rescue this fanciful, oversauced tale of stolen art and spiritual questioning.
item.dc:creator: Jeannette Catsoulis

item.title: Lin-Manuel Miranda’s ‘Warriors’ Musical to Hit Broadway Next Spring
item.description: Miranda is co-writing the musical — his first since “Hamilton” — with Eisa Davis. It’s based on “The Warriors” film and novel.
item.dc:creator: Michael Paulson
item.media:credit: Daniel Weiss for The New York Times
item.media:description: Lin-Manuel Miranda and Eisa Davis have jointly written the book, music and lyrics for their new musical, “Warriors.”

item.title: ‘The Sound of Music’ and ‘A Few Good Men’ Are Coming to Broadway
item.description: Lincoln Center Theater, basking in the glow of its Tony-winning “Ragtime” run, plans revivals of two more well-known titles this season.
item.dc:creator: Michael Paulson

item.title: Margaret Kerry, Body and Soul of Disney’s Tinker Bell, Dies at 97
item.description: A film crew and illustrator meticulously documented her pirouettes and pouts, giving substance to a character depicted previously as a spotlight onstage.
item.dc:creator: Alex Traub
item.media:description: Margaret Kerry in 2018. Trained as a dancer, she became the model for the animated version of Tinker Bell in the 1953 Disney movie “Peter Pan.”

item.title: Steve-O Has Beef With Father Time
item.description: “It sucks for anybody to get old,” the “Jackass” stunt performer says, “but for Steve-O, I feel like it is categorically not OK.”
item.dc:creator: Kathryn Shattuck
item.media:credit: Unique Nicole/Getty Images for Paramount Pictures

item.title: Why ‘Toy Story’ is the Best Franchise Ever
item.description: The New York Times film critic Alissa Wilkinson explores how the ‘Toy Story’ films leverage layers of nostalgia as a recipe for success.
item.dc:creator: Alissa Wilkinson, Edward Vega and Gabriel Blanco

item.title: James Bradley, Co-Author of ‘Flags of Our Fathers,’ Dies at 72
item.description: His best-selling book celebrated the servicemen in the stirring photograph of the U.S. flag-raising on Iwo Jima. One, it was long believed, was his father.
item.dc:creator: Richard Sandomir
item.media:description: James Bradley in 2006. His father, a Navy medic, spoke little of his time in combat, and Mr. Bradley sought to highlight his valor, including actions that earned him the Navy Cross.

item.title: Five Horror Movies to Stream Now
item.description: This month, victims are held hostage by demons, a deranged madman, the elements and a monstrosity named Hoagie.
item.dc:creator: Erik Piepenburg

item.title: 11 New Movies Our Critics Are Talking About This Week
item.description: Whether you’re a casual moviegoer or an avid buff, our reviewers think these films are worth knowing about.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: Five Free Movies to Stream Now
item.description: This month’s films, including “Citizenfour” and “The Candidate,” tell stories about America and our constant struggles to preserve freedom.
item.dc:creator: Brandon Yu
item.media:description: The whistle-blower Edward Snowden in “Citizenfour,” a documentary from Laura Poitras.

item.title: Billy Porter and Wayne Brady Make a Tender Pair in ‘La Cage aux Folles’
item.description: Billy Porter and Wayne Brady make a tender pair in Robert O’Hara’s revival of the Harvey Fierstein-Jerry Herman musical farce.
item.dc:creator: Laura Collins-Hughes
item.category: Encores! Great American Musicals in Concert (Series)
item.media:description: Wayne Brady, left, as Georges and Billy Porter as Albin in the Encores! production of “La Cage aux Folles” at New York City Center.

item.title: San Francisco Film Patrons Are Found Dead on Side of Highway
item.description: Judith Sheldon, 84, daughter of an Oscar-winning director, and her husband, Wylie, 86, were traveling to Oregon on a hot day. Their deaths are under investigation.
item.dc:creator: Heather Knight and Madaleine Rubin

item.title: ‘Voicemails for Isabelle’ Review: An Unexpected Recipient
item.description: The movie, starring Zoey Deutch and Nick Robinson, begins as a tear-jerker and morphs into a poignant rom-com.
item.dc:creator: Glenn Kenny

item.title: ‘Unidentified’ Review: A High-Concept Murder Mystery
item.description: The director Haifaa Al-Mansour (“Wadjda”) concludes a trilogy about women resisting societal constraints in Saudi Arabia with a whodunit.
item.dc:creator: Ben Kenigsberg

item.title: ‘Rose of Nevada’ Review: Out of the Mists of Time and Back Again
item.description: In this richly textured film by Mark Jenkin, two men sign on to crew a ship whose ultimate destination is a place they could never have fathomed.
item.dc:creator: Brandon Yu

item.title: ‘Maddie’s Secret’ Review: She’s Got the Sauce
item.description: A food influencer struggles with an eating disorder in John Early’s thrilling pastiche of the made-for-TV melodrama.
item.dc:creator: Natalia Winkelman

item.title: ‘Girls Like Girls’ Review: Hayley Kiyoko Brings Her Song to Life
item.description: The pop star Hayley Kiyoko turns her hit single and best-selling book into a coming-of-age romance.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Kennedy

item.title: ‘Leviticus’ Review: The Shocking Shapes That Love Can Take
item.description: In this Australian chiller, a conversion therapy ritual unleashes shape-shifting ghouls that take the form of desired ones.
item.dc:creator: Beatrice Loayza

item.title: ‘Color Book’ Review: Love and Grieving
item.description: In this tender family drama, a widowed father and his disabled son overcome personal tragedy.
item.dc:creator: Robert Daniels

item.title: ‘The Death of Robin Hood’ Review: Not a Merry Band
item.description: A darkly revisionist take on the medieval outlaw asks who gets to craft the heroic legends.
item.dc:creator: Alissa Wilkinson

item.title: ‘Never Change!’ Review: Failing the Assignment
item.description: This comedy squanders a promising premise about adults forced back to high school on repetitive sketches and hollow jokes.
item.dc:creator: Chris Azzopardi

item.title: ‘The Devil Queen’ Review: A Seedy Quest for Vengeance
item.description: Antonio Carlos da Fontoura’s 1974 gay gangster thriller returns in a gorgeous new restoration.
item.dc:creator: Erik Piepenburg

item.title: Critics of Russia Say This Critic Isn’t Critical Enough
item.description: Alexander Sokurov has questioned the Russian president about government repression, but he is still controversial among his country’s exiles.
item.dc:creator: Milana Mazaeva and Neil MacFarquhar
item.media:credit: Dmitry Kostyukov for The New York Times
item.media:description: Alexander Sokurov during the screening of his latest film, “Director’s Diary,” in Paris, in March.
